Lesson Candidates

  1. Before starting slow work or work involving tools, network calls, or delegation dispatch, send a short acknowledgement first, then proceed with the slow operation. skill_ready / safe / agent-runtime, dispatch, network, tooling
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-8107f53d4.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0
  2. If both automated script repair and AI-assisted repair have failed, avoid repeating the same repair loop and escalate to platform support. candidate / safe
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-8107f53d4.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0
  3. When a bot appears not to respond, check for delayed or running backend tool calls before declaring failure; if backend execution is not the cause, try another model to isolate the issue. candidate / safe
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-8107f53d4.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0
  4. When diagnosing chat-connector failures, verify the chat plugin is enabled, restart the service, and continue troubleshooting if the failure remains. candidate / safe
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-8107f53d4.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0
  5. Use issue categories such as installation or document integration, terminal session recovery, capacity or quota requests, bot visibility, and protocol-specific questions to choose an appropriate support path. candidate / safe
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-c721715b0.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0 source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-c721715b0.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_span_0
  6. When turning internal support notes into reusable documentation, remove personal routing, internal links, and project-specific identifiers while preserving the operational decision logic. skill_ready / safe
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-c721715b0.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0
  7. When answering model-related support questions, distinguish between pricing/capability differences, whether the user's selected model persists, and whether an agent-specific default may override that selection. candidate / safe
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-273a47187.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0
  8. For recurring context errors that occur after model switching, use a consistent explanation and troubleshooting guidance rather than improvising each response. candidate / safe
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-273a47187.json#src_openclaw_praxisbase-staging-experience-envelopes-experience-openclaw-answer-bot-sha256_json_0

Pending Experience Candidates

AI-generated wiki drafts waiting for review. Stable kb/ files are unchanged until promotion.

8

  1. ACK timing before long-running agent work

    Agents should send a brief acknowledgement before starting slow tool, network, dispatch, delegation, or other long-running work so users receive timely feedback before the operation begins.

    Target
    kb/known-fixes/ack-timing-before-long-running-agent-work.md
    Kind
    pitfall
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-14edce26a.json
    Sources
    1
    Confidence
    0.93
    Why review
    canonical_relationship
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    Do not treat an acknowledgement as a success report; it only confirms receipt or that work is starting.; Avoid exposing raw transcripts, credentials, tokens, cookies, authorization headers, or private keys in acknowledgement text, logs, or provenance.; If the platform already sends automatic receipt indicators, avoid redundant or noisy ACKs while still ensuring the user has timely feedback before slow work begins.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope
    Suggested links
    agents-can-mislead-users-if-failures-are-reported-as-successful-outcomes Agents can mislead users if failures are reported as successful outcomes. entity_overlap
    openclaw-dispatch-routing-failures OpenClaw dispatch routing failures entity_overlap
    openclaw-dispatch-routing-failures OpenClaw dispatch routing failures entity_overlap
    openclaw-slack-replay-and-post-deploy-stability-failures OpenClaw Slack replay and post-deploy stability failures entity_overlap
    Merge candidates
    ACK timing before long-running agent work kb/known-fixes/ack-timing-before-long-running-agent-work.md same_title_or_slug, entity_overlap
    Relationship reasons
    entity_overlap, same_title_or_slug

  2. OpenClaw dispatch routing failures

    Operational guidance for diagnosing OpenClaw/OctoClaw dispatch routing failures, including route mismatches, missing runners, spawn validation errors, and user-facing support routing pitfalls. Emphasizes dispatch-layer diagnosis, immediate acknowledgment for long-running work, transparent AI identity when human support is requested without details, and safe sanitization of routing documentation.

    Target
    kb/known-fixes/openclaw-dispatch-routing-failures.md
    Kind
    pitfall
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-1e5ae91e7.json, source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-c721715b0.json
    Sources
    2
    Confidence
    0.94
    Why review
    ambiguous_merge_target; multiple_canonical_targets
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    Existing page content contained more specific operational examples than the new evidence; these were preserved where broadly useful but kept at a non-sensitive level.; No relationship links were supplied for this compilation, so no new wiki links were added.; Guidance involving support routing was generalized to avoid exposing private escalation paths or internal identifiers.; quality_human_required:missing_wikilinks; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope; quality_human_required:ambiguous_merge_target
    Merge candidates
    OpenClaw dispatch routing failures kb/known-fixes/openclaw-dispatch-routing-failures.md same_title_or_slug
    OpenClaw dispatch routing failures skills/openclaw/openclaw-dispatch-routing-failures/SKILL.md same_title_or_slug
    Relationship reasons
    same_title_or_slug

  3. Users may report that an interactive environment or bot is down, sleeping, or unresponsive

    For a down, sleeping, or unresponsive interactive environment or bot, first direct the user to the product console’s automatic repair option, unless the issue appears related to billing, quota, or payment. If both script-based and AI-based repair have already failed, escalate instead of repeating repair steps.

    Target
    kb/known-fixes/users-may-report-that-an-interactive-environment-or-bot-is-down-sleeping-or.md
    Kind
    known_fix
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-19c2eeefa.json
    Sources
    1
    Confidence
    0.93
    Why review
    new_canonical_topic
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    The evidence does not name a specific product console or repair UI path, so keep instructions generic unless product-specific documentation is available.; Do not apply this flow to billing, quota, or payment-related sleep conditions.; Escalate rather than repeating repair steps when both script-based and AI-based repair have already failed.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope

  4. Bypassing Authorization Failures Can Violate Access Controls, Privacy Expectations, or System Policy

    When authorized document retrieval fails, agents must not seek mirrors, cached copies, alternate accounts, or indirect access paths. Stop retrieval attempts and ask the requester to provide access, paste the content, or upload it through an approved channel.

    Target
    kb/pitfalls/bypassing-authorization-failures-can-violate-access-controls-privacy.md
    Kind
    pitfall
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-dd5113c71.json
    Sources
    1
    Confidence
    0.93
    Why review
    new_canonical_topic
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    This page is intentionally conservative: it treats authorization failures as boundaries and does not provide bypass techniques.; Agents should still follow higher-priority system, organizational, or product-specific policy if it gives stricter instructions.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope

  5. Agents may provide unreliable or hallucinated answers about their own runtime model or configuration

    Use an authoritative runtime/session status mechanism, not an agent's natural-language self-report, to verify the active model or runtime configuration.

    Target
    kb/procedures/agents-may-provide-unreliable-or-hallucinated-answers-about-their-own-runtime.md
    Kind
    procedure
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-e437220d9.json
    Sources
    1
    Confidence
    0.93
    Why review
    new_canonical_topic
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    Specific status command names vary by platform; use the local authoritative runtime/session status mechanism when `system/status` is unavailable.; Do not expose raw runtime metadata if it contains sensitive operational details; report only the configuration facts needed for the task.; If no authoritative source is available, avoid asserting a specific active model based only on agent self-report.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope

  6. Reply Inside an Existing Threaded Chat Conversation

    Before sending a chat reply, determine whether the interaction belongs inside an existing thread. If it does, include the platform-required parent message identifier so the reply appears in the thread instead of the main channel or group timeline.

    Target
    kb/procedures/reply-inside-existing-threaded-chat-conversation.md
    Kind
    procedure
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-31384b954.json
    Sources
    1
    Confidence
    0.93
    Why review
    new_canonical_topic
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    Platform-specific field names vary; agents must consult the active platform adapter or API contract for the exact parent/root message identifier field.; Attaching an incorrect parent message identifier can misroute a reply to the wrong thread.; Do not fabricate or reuse parent identifiers when the current interaction is not part of an existing thread.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope

  7. Verify Effective Runtime Model After a Reported Model Switch

    Agents or UIs may report that a model switch succeeded even when the active runtime model is still different from the intended model. Confirm model changes with an explicit session status, diagnostic, or configuration command before relying on the new model.

    Target
    kb/procedures/the-agent-or-ui-may-report-success-even-when-the-effective-runtime-model.md
    Kind
    procedure
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-dbfb119fc.json
    Sources
    1
    Confidence
    0.93
    Why review
    new_canonical_topic
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    Specific verification commands are platform-dependent and must be selected from the relevant system documentation or available interface.; If no explicit status or configuration inspection is available, the active model cannot be conclusively verified from self-report alone.; Additional verification may be unnecessary for low-risk exploratory interactions where exact model identity has no operational impact.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope

  8. Verify Session-Level Configuration Changes Before Relying on Them

    Treat session-level configuration or behavior changes as temporary until the active configuration is explicitly inspected in the current session or worker context.

    Target
    kb/procedures/verify-session-level-configuration-changes-before-relying-on-them.md
    Kind
    procedure
    Scope
    team
    Source
    source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-745fa543d.json
    Sources
    1
    Confidence
    0.93
    Why review
    new_canonical_topic
    Suggested
    edit
    Risk notes
    Source evidence is a single lesson, so the procedure is intentionally general rather than tied to a specific tool or command.; No raw credentials, tokens, cookies, auth headers, private keys, or sensitive transcript content were included.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope
